steps.title: Identify the y-intercept location
steps.content: The y-intercept is the point where a line crosses the y-axis. This always occurs when $x = 0$. On a graph, we look at where the line meets the vertical axis.

steps.title: Read the y-coordinate from the graph
steps.content: Locating where the line intersects the y-axis in the graph, the crossing point is at $y = 8$. Therefore, the y-intercept is the point $(0, 8)$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
